founder.bio: For the last 10+ years I have helped Bangladeshi founders turn ideas into real, profitable e-commerce brands. I started with design, grew into branding, and now run a full-stack growth studio focused on AI marketing systems. My mission: make world-class e-commerce education and execution accessible to every ambitious founder in Bangladesh.

about.title: About Saiful Studios
about.subtitle: Built on a mission to democratize e-commerce success for every Bangladeshi entrepreneur.
about.storyTitle: From one designer to a full growth studio
about.storyP1: Saiful Islam started as a freelance designer over 10 years ago, helping brands look great. He realized great design alone does not build a business – strategy, marketing, and systems do.
about.storyP2: After years of working with international clients, he founded Saiful Studios to help Bangladeshi entrepreneurs build sustainable, profitable online businesses.
about.storyP3: Today, Saiful Studios is the go-to partner for e-commerce entrepreneurs across Bangladesh.

winningProducts.title: How to Find Winning Products to Sell in Bangladesh (2026 Guide)
winningProducts.excerpt: Finding the right product is the #1 reason most Bangladeshi e-commerce businesses fail before they even start.
winningProducts.content:
Finding the right product is the #1 reason most Bangladeshi e-commerce businesses fail before they even start. In this guide, I will walk you through the exact framework we use with our Academy students and Growth Partner clients.

**Step 1: Start with Google Trends (Bangladesh filter)**

Use Google Trends with Bangladesh as the country. Look for products that are trending UP, not the ones that already peaked. The sweet spot is "rising" products that have 50–200% growth in the last 6 months.

**Step 2: Validate on Facebook Ad Library**

Go to Facebook Ad Library and search for the product. If you see 10+ active ads from different brands running for more than 2 weeks, that is a strong signal the product is profitable.

**Step 3: Use Gemini for competitor analysis**

Ask Gemini to summarize recent news, reviews, and Reddit/forum discussions about the product category. You will quickly see the common pain points and what customers actually want.

**Step 4: Test with a ৳5,000 ad budget**

Run a 5-day test campaign on Facebook with 3 creatives. If your cost-per-purchase is under ৳300, scale. If not, kill the product and try the next one.

**Step 5: Source and launch**

Once validated, source from Alibaba, IndiaMart, or local wholesale markets in Dhaka (Imam Square, Tantibazar). Aim for a 3x markup after shipping and ad costs.

Following this process, our students have launched 500+ products in the last 18 months. About 20% become profitable, which is the industry standard for new product testing.
